title: Easter Sunday at the Wilderness
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
April 17, 2022 | "Easter Sunday at the Wilderness" | Preacher: Broderick Greer

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is Easter Sunday (The Feast of the Resurrection), the highest holy day in the Christian calendar and the culmination of Holy Week. The lectionary readings for Easter Sunday 2022 under the Revised Common Lectionary would be Acts 10:34-43 (Peter's speech at Cornelius's house), Psalm 118:1-2, 14-24, 1 Corinthians 15:1-11 (Paul on the Resurrection), and John 20:1-18 (Mary Magdalene at the empty tomb). This marks the beginning of Eastertide, a 50-day liturgical season extending toward Pentecost, and represents the transition from the penitential season of Lent into the joy of Resurrection faith.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the midst of preparations for General Convention scheduled for Baltimore in July 2022, where significant debates over LGBTQ+ inclusion and prayer book revision would take place. The Diocese of Colorado had recently navigated pandemic-related transitions in worship and was engaged in ongoing conversations about reopening and in-person ministry. Broderick Greer, who had been ordained as a deacon in the Episcopal Church, was part of a younger generation of Black Anglican clergy gaining visibility within the denomination during this period of racial reckoning conversations.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was emerging from winter weather patterns with spring conditions establishing themselves by mid-April 2022. The city was focused on local elections and municipal governance following the previous year's mayoral campaign, with ongoing attention to homelessness, affordable housing, and post-pandemic recovery in the downtown Capitol Hill corridor. Saint John's Cathedral, located in downtown Denver, was serving a diverse urban congregation navigating the transition to fuller in-person worship after two years of pandemic disruptions.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The primary national storyline was the ongoing war in Ukraine following Russia's February 2022 invasion, with U.S. military aid packages and NATO concerns dominating headlines. The domestic political environment remained polarized around Biden administration policies, with midterm elections in November 2022 looming and inflation becoming an increasingly salient economic concern. COVID-19 remained present but was no longer the dominant national narrative, with most restrictions lifted and vaccination debates continuing in pockets of American life.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's invasion of Ukraine was entering its third month with intense fighting in eastern Ukraine and ongoing humanitarian crises, including reports of atrocities in Bucha that had emerged in early April 2022. China was implementing new COVID lockdowns in Shanghai and other major cities, creating global supply chain concerns. The broader international mood reflected Cold War anxieties and humanitarian concern for Eastern European populations displaced by active military conflict.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The American cultural moment in April 2022 reflected post-pandemic normalization mixed with war anxiety and economic uncertainty about inflation. The Oscars ceremony had just occurred on March 27, dominated by discussion of the Will Smith incident, providing a moment of celebrity scandal distraction from weightier concerns. For Denver specifically and religious communities nationally, Easter 2022 represented a return to fuller in-person celebration after two years of pandemic-altered worship, carrying particular symbolic weight around themes of resurrection, renewal, and hope amid ongoing global uncertainty.
